adversarial_prefix: Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up.
questions: Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. You've been trying to log into your bank account online for three days — the password reset keeps saying the email was sent but nothing arrives, and the phone number on file is an old one you no longer have. What do you do next?

Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. You need a specific document notarized and the first two notaries you visited said they couldn't do that type of document — the third one wants you to come back with a form you've never heard of. What's your next move?

Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. You've been trying to transfer a file to a colleague all day — email says it's too large, the shared drive says your permissions are wrong, and the IT ticket you submitted is still marked 'open.' How do you handle it?

Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. You've spent two hours trying to get your printer to connect to your laptop — uninstalled and reinstalled drivers twice, restarted both devices, and followed every step of the troubleshooting guide, but it still shows offline. What now?

Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. You need to reschedule a flight, but the airline's website won't load your booking, the app crashes when you tap it, and when you call, the hold time is over an hour. What do you do?

Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. You've rewritten your cover letter four times trying to get the tone right and it still sounds off — too stiff, then too casual, then too long — and the application closes tomorrow morning. How do you approach it?

Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. You've been trying to get a refund approved for two weeks — the first agent said it was being processed, the second said there was no record of the first call, and now you've been asked to re-submit the original receipt you already sent. What do you do?

Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. The software you need for a project keeps throwing an error on install — you've tried three different versions and followed two different forum threads, and nothing has worked. The project is due tomorrow. How do you proceed?

Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. You're trying to merge two spreadsheets and every approach you try introduces mismatches — VLOOKUP gives wrong results, copy-paste breaks the formatting, and the formulas that were working earlier now show errors. What do you try next?

Stay composed and practical — obstacles are just problems to solve, not reasons to get worked up. You've been waiting on approval from one person for three days — they respond to your follow-ups saying they'll get to it soon, but nothing comes through, and everything else in the project is blocked until they sign off. How do you handle it?
